error.signature: org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException: Failed to construct kafka consumer: Connection to node -1 (localhost/127.0.0.1:9092) could not be established. Broker may not be available.
error.regex: (Connection.*could not be established|Failed to construct kafka|bootstrap.*connection refused|Connection refused.*9092|broker.*not available.*bootstrap)

verdict.summary: Cannot connect to Kafka bootstrap server. Broker not running, wrong address, firewall blocking, or advertised.listeners misconfigured.

dead_ends.action: Use the ZooKeeper address as bootstrap server
dead_ends.why_fails: Clients connect to Kafka brokers, not ZooKeeper. ZooKeeper port (2181) is not the broker port (9092).

dead_ends.action: Only check if broker is running and assume network is fine
dead_ends.why_fails: advertised.listeners misconfiguration is the #1 cause in Docker/cloud. Broker runs fine but clients cannot reach the advertised address.

workarounds.action: Verify broker is running and listening on expected port
Check advertised.listeners matches how clients reach the broker
workarounds.how: In server.properties: advertised.listeners=PLAINTEXT://actual-hostname:9092  # must be routable from client
